moves.note: Add quality tech, strong scanner read

plan: Sell all leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) to eliminate high risk.
Increase allocation to income ETF (QQQI) for stability.
Trim broad index (SPY) to reduce concentration.
Buy Microsoft (MSFT) as a top quality tech addition.
Add to AI software (CRWD) to boost a strong performer.
This plan prioritizes capital preservation with reduced leverage.
conservative.read: This plan aims for slow, steady growth around 1.5% per month. It significantly reduces risk by cutting leverage and focusing on stable assets, leading to lower potential drawdowns.

moves.note: Add quality tech, strong scanner read
Add strong tech with good R/R

plan: Trim leveraged ETF (SOXL) to a minimal position.
Exit the smallest leveraged ETF (SPAL).
Trim broad index (SPY) to meet concentration limits.
Buy Microsoft (MSFT) for quality tech exposure.
Add NVDU based on its strong risk/reward setup.
Increase AI software (CRWD) to leverage its strength.
balanced.read: This balanced approach targets about 2.5% monthly growth. It reduces risk by trimming leverage and concentration while adding quality names, aiming for steady growth with controlled drawdowns.

moves.note: Add quality tech, strong scanner read

plan: Increase allocation to leveraged semiconductor ETF (SOXL).
Exit the smallest leveraged ETF (SPAL).
Trim broad index (SPY) to reduce concentration.
Add NVDU, the top-rated tech setup from scanner.
Buy Microsoft (MSFT) for its strong scanner signal.
Increase AI software (CRWD) to leverage its strength.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets ~4% monthly growth by leaning into leverage and concentration. It accepts significantly higher potential drawdowns and the risk of amplified losses due to leverage and daily rebalancing of ETFs.

moves.note: Watch quality tech for income add

plan: Sell all high-risk leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL).
Sell high-beta software (CRWD) to reduce volatility.
Maximize allocation to income ETF (QQQI).
Increase broad index (SPY) for stability and dividends.
Keep cash buffer high for distributions.
Focus on steady monthly distributions over growth.
income.read: This income-focused plan aims for a low ~1% monthly return primarily through distributions. It prioritizes capital preservation and stable cash flow, accepting minimal growth and very low drawdowns.
